events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario involves equipment bonding on an industrial site, with isolated metal parts observed during in-progress work. NEC 250.104 requires bonding of metal piping and structural steel; NEC 250.30 covers separately derived systems grounding and bonding. Isolated metal parts that should be bonded create a shock/arc hazard. This is non-compliant and potentially installed-defective.

  "findings": [
    "Isolated metal parts lack required bonding to the equipment grounding/bonding system, creating potential for energized metal under fault conditions.",
    "Bonding of metal piping/structural metal parts not established per NEC 250.104.",
    "Separately derived system bonding/grounding not verified per NEC 250.30 where applicable."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work in the affected area and isolate/lock out associated circuits until bonding is corrected.",
    "Install approved bonding jumpers or permanent bonding connections to all isolated metal parts per engineered drawings and NEC 250.104.",
    "Verify separately derived system grounding and bonding electrode conductor connections per NEC 250.30.",
    "Continuity test the bonding path before energizing any equipment.",
    "Document the deficiency and notify the electrical inspector/engineer of record."
  ],
  "rationale": "Isolated metal parts on an industrial site must be bonded to the grounding/bonding system to prevent differences of potential and shock/arc hazards under fault conditions. The condition is non-compliant with NEC 250.104 and potentially 250.30, and is an installed-defective state while work is in progress.",
  "references": ["NEC 250.104", "NEC 250.30"]
}
